items.title: UMAP
items.description: Another non-linear embedding for visualization that often preserves more global structure than t-SNE and scales better; compare layouts when choosing an embedding method.

items.title: Embedding visualization
items.description: Best practices for plotting, coloring, and interpreting 2D/3D embeddings—t-SNE output should be read with those visualization guidelines.

items.title: Principal Component Analysis
items.description: PCA is linear, fast, and preserves global variance; t-SNE is non-linear and tuned for local neighborhoods. PCA-to-50D is a common t-SNE preprocessor—see PCA for that linear step.

items.title: Singular Value Decomposition
items.description: When t-SNE uses init='pca' or a PCA preprocessing step, SVD of centered data under the hood supplies the starting subspace—see SVD for the factorization, not re-derived here.

items.title: Curse of dimensionality
items.description: High-dimensional data is hard to visualize directly; t-SNE is one response that projects to 2D/3D for human inspection of structure that distance geometry obscures in raw p dimensions.

items.title: K-means clustering
items.description: t-SNE plots often precede or accompany clustering EDA; k-means on original features or PCA scores is separate from the t-SNE layout, which distorts global geometry.
